message:
fix: multiple regex compilation and test bugs

- Fix regex compilation errors by compiling patterns at init time
  instead of inside functions (cli/bind.go, context/monitor.go,
  history/entry.go, quota/claude.go, templates/parser.go,
  tmux/session.go, tools/bv.go)
- Fix watcher poll mode recursive bug - subdirectories weren't
  being tracked in watchedPaths when using fallback polling
- Fix watcher test to accept poll mode fallback as valid behavior
- Fix resilience monitor_test.go race condition - properly wait
  for async restart before checking restart count
- Remove unnecessary regex recompilation in pipeline/executor.go
  and pipeline/parser.go (use existing package-level patterns)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -10,7 +10,6 @@ import (
 	"log"

@@ -1678,8 +1677,7 @@ func (vc *VariableContext) SetVariable(name string, value interface{}) {
 
 // EvaluateString evaluates all variable references in a string
 func (vc *VariableContext) EvaluateString(s string) string {
-	varPattern := regexp.MustCompile(`\$\{([^}]+)\}`)
-
+	// Uses package-level varPattern from variables.go
 	return varPattern.ReplaceAllStringFunc(s, func(match string) string {
 		ref := match[2 : len(match)-1]
 		if val, ok := vc.GetVariable(ref); ok {

-	varRefPattern := regexp.MustCompile(`\$\{([^}]+)\}`)
-
+	// Uses package-level varPattern from variables.go
 	checkString := func(s, field string) {
-		matches := varRefPattern.FindAllStringSubmatch(s, -1)
+		matches := varPattern.FindAllStringSubmatch(s, -1)
 		for _, match := range matches {
 			ref := match[1]
 			parts := strings.Split(ref, ".")
